trans-unit.source: 📋 This is a detached log window. It will update in real-time.
Don't show this wizard on startup
Files in multi-file project (click for details)
Always-on voice listening status Click to toggle
Import a Word document (DOCX), Adobe InDesign markup (IDML), HTML, XLIFF, PO, Excel (XLSX), or PowerPoint (PPTX). Extracted via the Okapi sidecar; round-trips back to the original format on export.
Import a GNU gettext message catalogue (Linux/Django/WordPress software localisation)
Re-import an edited Supervertaler bilingual Word table to update this project's translations and comments.
Re-import an edited [SEGMENT NNNN] text file (with its .svexport.json sidecar) to update the current project's translations and comments.
Open the Supported File Formats reference in your browser
Export the translation back to a document. Output format is the same as the original source (DOCX → DOCX, IDML → IDML, etc.). Uses the Okapi sidecar to preserve formatting.
Export segments as a one-way bilingual Markdown table for AI translation/review (not re-importable)
Export the project back to a GNU gettext .po catalogue, preserving comments, references and flags
Export multi-file project to folder with separate files
Repoint to moved/renamed source folder for multi-file project
Export a re-importable Word bilingual table (inline tags shown as literal text). Edit the Target/Comments columns, then re-import to update this project.
Export a re-importable [SEGMENT NNNN] text file with a .svexport.json sidecar. Edit the target lines (or have an LLM do it), then re-import to update this project.
Translate segments with empty target (useful after partial batch translation)
Translate only segments currently visible after filtering
Copy source text to target for selected/filtered segments
Copy source to target for segments containing no letters (numbers, codes, punctuation only) and mark as Translated

Send/update confirmed (and other selected-status) segments into a writable Translation Memory. With overwrite on, same-source entries are replaced so the TM keeps only the latest translation. (Formerly 'Send Segments to TM'.)
Remove formatting tags from selected segments
Use AI to proofread and verify translation quality
Dock the panel above the translation grid
Dock the panel below the translation grid
Automatically resize all rows to fit content
Private notes for this project (never exported to CAT tools)
Open the session log in a separate window that can be moved to another screen
Open help for the focused panel (F1)
Open the Workbench documentation in your browser
Run the initial setup wizard (data folder location, features overview)
Check whether you are running the latest Supervertaler release
Copy version and system info to clipboard (useful for support)
Open the log file containing startup output and errors – useful when reporting issues
Reveal the folder that contains the diagnostic log files
Switch the right panel back to the Prompt Editor
Create an 'Images' folder next to each DOCX file

No image selected
Click on a file in the list to preview or Extract images to see them here
Auto: 'Images' folder next to each DOCX
⚠️ No valid images found in folder

📚 Supervertaler Help is now available online.
Visit https://help.supervertaler.com/workbench/ to view the documentation.
Open log in a separate window that can be moved to another screen
Re-attach SuperLookup to the Home tab

Translation Memories Manager
Database not initialized.
Manage TMs. Activate/deactivate TMs for current project. Import client TMX files as named TMs.
Uncheck the Read flag on every TM
Uncheck the Write flag on every TM
Uncheck the Bridge flag on every TM (hide all TMs from the Trados plugin)
Read: TM is used for matching segments
Write: TM is updated with new translations
Import TMX file as a new TM or add to existing TM
Export selected TM to TMX file
Open editor for selected TM (Browse, Import/Export, Maintenance)
Import translations by matching base languages, ignoring regional variants
Create a separate TM preserving the exact language variants from the TMX
Manage language-specific segmentation rules for accurate sentence/segment boundaries.
Test current segmentation rules on sample text
Import segmentation rules from file (Coming Soon)
Manage termbases for terminology searching. Activate/deactivate for current project.
Uncheck the Read flag on every termbase
Uncheck the Write flag on every termbase
Select a termbase to view/edit terms
Mark term as forbidden (do not use)
Read: Termbase is used for terminology matching
Write: Termbase is updated with new terms
Set as Project Termbase (highest priority, pink)
AI: Send termbase terms to LLM with translation prompts
Extract terminology from project segments to create project termbase
Extract terminology from project source segments to create a project termbase.
Create a new project termbase with selected terms
Create new TM from this TMX
Set your default source and target languages for translation projects.
Select your preferred AI translation provider:
Choose the specific model to use:
Add a new custom API profile
API key for this endpoint (leave empty to use api_keys.txt)
Uncheck providers you don't want to use. Only enabled providers will be available.
ⓘ Surrounding segments sent to the AI for context. 0 = no context. Default: 5
Larger batches = faster but higher API cost per call
ⓘ Larger batches = faster but higher API cost. Default: 20
Include surrounding context in batch translation
Check TM before calling AI (saves API costs):
Don't check TM - always call AI
ⓘ Prevents searches while navigating quickly. Default: 1500ms
Enable LLM (AI) matching on segment selection
Select which MT engines to query. Only enabled providers with valid API keys are shown.
Add a new Custom MT endpoint profile
model / engine name (e.g. google, sogou, cnpat)
Auto-fetch AI providers in the popup (otherwise show 'Fetch' buttons)
Use your custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint (configured in AI Settings)
Uses active profile from AI Settings
Use a locally-running Ollama model for translation (no API key needed)
Ollama model name – must be pulled locally (ollama pull <model>)
When enabled, Supervertaler will automatically open the last project you were working on when the application starts.
When enabled, the log window will automatically open in a separate detached window when Supervertaler starts.
↻ Restart Supervertaler for the new language to take effect.
Share anonymous usage statistics (no personal data)
Your translation memories, termbases, prompts, and settings are stored here:
Choose a different location for your data folder
Open this folder in your file manager
💡 This folder persists across updates. Back it up regularly!
💡 Uses Windows system beeps (no audio files).
Auto-fill empty segments with 100% TM matches
When you select an empty segment that has a 100% TM match, its translation is filled in automatically.
Otherwise the auto-filled translation is inserted as a draft for you to review.
Auto-propagate confirmed translations to identical segments
Otherwise propagated translations are inserted as drafts for you to review.
↳ Also overwrite existing translations with 100% TM matches
Highlight termbase matches in source cells
Hide shorter termbase matches included in longer ones
Disable ALL caches (direct lookups every time)
Select the font family for grid text
Configure how content is displayed in the translation grid.
Hide outer wrapping tags in grid (e.g. <li-o>...</li-o>)
Show Status column between Source and Target (uncheck to move it to the far right)
Set font sizes for the Match Panel (TM/termbase matches) and tag colors.
Show HTML/XML tags in match text
When enabled, tags like <b>, <li>, etc. are displayed. When disabled, only the text content is shown.
Color for CAT tool tags (e.g. <b>, [uicontrol], {MQ}) in the grid and results pane
Color for match number badges in Translation Results panel
Color for invisible character symbols (·→↵); non-breaking spaces are shaded with this colour
When enabled, even and odd rows have different background colors
Reset to white (#FFFFFF) and light gray (#F0F0F0)
Customize the border that appears around the target cell when it has focus.
Color of the focus border around target cells
Thickness of the focus border (1-10 pixels)
Traditional highlight with pastel green background colors based on priority
Dotted Underline - Subtle dotted line below text (IDE style)
Unobtrusive dotted underline like code editors use for suggestions
Semibold Text - Slightly bolder text with tinted color (default)
Typographic approach: text appears slightly heavier/darker without adding visual elements
Color for dotted underline (soft blue-grey recommended)
Configure the font appearance for the TermLens inline terminology display.
Select the font family for TermLens text
Configure the font appearance for the TM Source and TM Target boxes in the Match Panel.
Select the font family for TM Source/Target text in the Match Panel
Match Panel TM font size (6-18 pt). Can also be adjusted with View → Match Panel zoom shortcuts.
Display TM Source/Target text in bold
Compact UI chrome (tighter menu / tab padding)
Tighten padding on the menu bar and tab strips to make more room for the translation grid
Always-on voice listening ACTIVE Click to stop
Voice – Always-On is OFF. Click to start.
Supervertaler uses different system prompts for different translation modes:
Select which system prompt to view/edit
Edit the system prompt below. Use {{SOURCE_LANGUAGE}}, {{TARGET_LANGUAGE}}, and {{SOURCE_TEXT}} as placeholders.
Restore the default system prompt for this mode
Export the current debug log buffer to a timestamped file
Clear the in-memory debug log buffer
Legal/Notarial Documents: Notarial deeds, contracts, court documents, Belgian law
Medical Documents: Clinical reports, procedures, pharmaceutical documents
Patent Documents: Patent applications, claims, prior art references
Reset all keyword lists to default values
View settings have been saved and applied successfully.
Filter segments by file (multi-file projects only)
Select text first to wrap it with a tag pair (e.g., selection)
Compact Tag View Shortens verbose tags like <bmk id="0" ...> to {1}
Preview the complete assembled prompt (System Prompt + Custom Prompts + current segment)
Push-to-talk dictation – press your dictation shortcut (or click) to record, transcribe, and insert text. Set the key in Settings → Keyboard Shortcuts.
Always-On listening – continuously monitors the mic and transcribes automatically No need to press the dictation shortcut
Paste or type your source text below:
You can add segments manually after creation.
Use source text for untranslated segments
Language code for source text (e.g., NL, EN, DE)
Language code for target text (e.g., EN, NL, FR)
Detect memoQ bilingual format (DOCX files with tables)
If checked, DOCX files with bilingual tables will be imported as memoQ bilingual format
Original Format - Each file exports to its source format
Plain Text (.txt) - One line per segment
Markdown (.md) - One line per segment (preserves syntax)
Bold, italic, and underline formatting will not be transferred to translations. Use this if formatting causes issues or isn't needed.
When checked, formatting (bold/italic/underline) will not be applied to translations.
Attempts to identify formatted text in source and apply matching formatting to corresponding words in the translation.
When checked, tries to match formatted source phrases to their translations.
ℹ️ Note: Inline XML tags (like <b>text</b>) are always preserved regardless of this setting.
No translatable strings found in the .po file.

Successfully imported {n} segment(s) from gettext .po.
Languages: {src} → {tgt}
Pretranslated: {n} segment(s) with target text loaded.
Fuzzy: {n} segment(s) marked draft (clear the fuzzy flag by editing).

To export to gettext .po format, please select the original .po (or .pot) file.
This is the file you originally imported.
Would you like to select it now?

Successfully exported {n} translated segment(s) to gettext .po.
File: {name}
ℹ️ Note: Déjà Vu tags {NNNNN} will be highlighted in the translation grid.
💡 Tip: Press F5 to refresh if matches are missing
✏️ Edit TM Entry (no TM ID available)
🗑️ Delete TM Entry (no TM ID available)
Select one or more rows in the grid to enable this option
Double-click any row to navigate to that segment in the grid.
Update existing entries (overwrite if source matches)
If a segment with the same source text exists, update its target.
Click 'Preview' to see how many segments will be sent.
F&R Sets: Save and Run Multiple Find and Replace Operations
Add current Find/Replace to the selected set
Enter custom words here, one per line...
2. Place them in the dictionaries folder
⚠️ Not initialized - try changing language
Copy version and system info to clipboard
Java needed for enhanced file filters
📖 TM (Translation Memory) - Pre-translate from activated TMs (exact + fuzzy ≥75%)
⚡ Exact matches only (100% matches only - fastest)
🤖 LLM (AI) - Use configured LLM provider (GPT, Claude, Gemini)
⚠️ Configure MT API keys in Settings → MT Settings to enable
🔄 Retry until all segments are translated (recommended)
Enter search term or paste text...
Filter by source language (leave as 'Any' for all)
Filter by target language (leave as 'Any' for all)
Ready. Select a mode and capture text to begin.
Source and Target side-by-side in columns
Source above Target, like traditional concordance
Add source text and selected translation as new term
💡 Tip: Double-click a term to copy translation to clipboard
Enter text and click Search to get MT translations
Search all web resources at once (embedded mode)
Open current page in your default web browser
Languages: Any → Any • Click Search above or select a resource
Please enter a search term in the Source Text field above
Enter a search term in the Source Text field first
Configure which resources to search. Each resource type has its own tab below.

🚧 Under Construction
Coming soon: • DeepL integration • Google Translate • Microsoft Translator • Amazon Translate • ModernMT
💡 Tip: More web resources can be added in future updates
⚠️ No text to search. Enter or capture text first.
No MT results. Check provider settings.
Opens the AutoHotkey download page in your browser
Locate AutoHotkey.exe if you have it installed in a custom location
💡 Note: After installing AutoHotkey, restart Supervertaler to enable the global hotkey.
Do not show this dialog again
